content.content:
1	# Cross-Session Learnings — JasonVaughanComPortfolio
2	
3	Non-obvious behaviors, validated patterns, and failure modes worth remembering. Skip the obvious; capture only what would change how a future session approaches a similar task.
4	
5	## 2026-06-22 — Adding a ccusage-backed token source (Codex): three non-obvious traps
6	
7	Wiring OpenAI Codex into the stats collector surfaced three things that would have bitten a future "add another agent's tokens" task. See project-assets #28/#29/#30.
8	
9	- **ccusage's multi-agent support is per-package, not per-subcommand on the installed binary.** The README lists ~15 agents (Codex, OpenClaw, Copilot CLI…), but the globally-installed `ccusage` (20.0.14) only has the Claude Code source — `ccusage codex` errors with "Command not found". Codex support lives in `ccusage@latest` (or the deprecated `@ccusage/codex`). **Don't trust the README's capability list against the installed version — probe the actual binary, and standardize on `npx -y ccusage@latest <agent>`.**
10	
11	- **`auth_mode` is the double-count discriminator for any agent that can auth two ways.** Codex via ChatGPT login (`auth_mode=chatgpt`) is subscription-billed and *invisible* to the OpenAI admin-API usage report → safely additive. API-key auth *is* in that report → adding it would double-count. The fix that turns this from a fragile comment into a contract: gate collection on `auth_mode` per machine (only count when `chatgpt`), so a future auth switch can't silently double-count. Generalizes to any new token source — check its billing path before stacking it on an existing `api` source.
12	
13	- **A "deployed copy with a manual sync step" *will* drift — verify the scheduled artifact is the one you edited.** The launchd agent ran a hand-copied `~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh` snapshot, not the repo script; editing `local-agent/refresh.sh` in the repo did nothing on schedule (the copy had frozen out the Codex source for 2 days). The durable fix is a **self-deploying wrapper** (`run-agent.sh`: `git pull` → `install` script to a stable path → `exec` the copy). It also dodges the mid-run-mutation hazard of pointing launchd directly at a repo file that the script itself `git pull`s. Before assuming a local fix is live, confirm what the scheduler actually executes.
14	
15	## 2026-06-24 — Collapsible page + deep-links: five traps shipped across PRs #83/#84/#85
16	
17	Converting the whole page to collapsible dropdowns (+ per-card/section share links) surfaced things that would re-bite any "collapse sections" or "deep-link to a card" task.
18	
19	- **Collapsing by *unmounting* silently breaks deep-links into nested content.** First pass unmounted closed sections (clean for animation), but then `#monad-1` (a card inside Research) had no DOM element to scroll to. Fix: keep collapsed content **mounted**, hide via CSS (`grid-template-rows: 0fr->1fr` + `inert`), and have the hash handler walk *up* from the target to its enclosing `[data-collapsible]`, open that, then scroll+flash. Always-mounted is also just the original page behavior -> no perf regression. Don't unmount anything a deep-link might target.
20	- **Wrapping a component that owns its own `id` in a wrapper that also sets that `id` = duplicate DOM ids (12 sections).** Critic caught it. Fix: carry the section id in a `data-collapsible="<id>"` *attribute* (the registry/handler reads it), and only render an actual DOM `id` on the wrapper when the inner content has none (`provideId`/`bodyInWrap`). Edge case that broke the first fix: Pipeline renders **no** inner id, so omitting the wrapper id left `#pipeline` with no target — needed an explicit `provideId`. Rule: exactly one element per id; decide where the id lives based on whether the child already provides it.
21	- **A passing "pulse class is applied" test masked an invisible flash.** The amber highlight is a `box-shadow` ring — visible on a bordered card, but on a large transparent `<section>` the ring sits at the page edges and reads as nothing. `pulsed=true` in Playwright was true yet the user saw no flash. For section-level links, flash a *defined* element (the dropdown **header panel**), not the section. Lesson: assert the *visible effect*, not just that the class/attr was applied.
22	- **`navigator.clipboard` is blocked on insecure origins** (plain-HTTP LAN/tailnet, e.g. the `100.x:3300` dev server) — `writeText` rejects and the copy button silently no-ops. Needs a secure-context guard + legacy `execCommand("copy")` textarea fallback. Recurring trap for anything tested over the tailnet rather than `localhost`/HTTPS.
23	- **Two git/dev-serving gotchas:** (1) Branching a follow-up fix off a branch that then got **squash-merged** makes the next PR `DIRTY` (it re-applies the already-merged changes) — fix with `git rebase --onto origin/main <old-tip> <branch>` to replay only the new commits. (2) `vite dev` serves the SPA fallback for `/share/<id>/` (trailing slash) but the real OG stub only at `/share/<id>/index.html`; the trailing-slash form looks broken locally while GitHub Pages serves it correctly — don't diagnose the share-stub redirect from a `vite dev` trailing-slash URL.
24	
25	**Validated tooling:** for DOM/scroll/anchor/flash behavior that vitest (node, no jsdom) can't cover, a throwaway **Playwright** script driving the real dev server is the right verification — it caught the duplicate id, the scroll near-miss, and the `/share/` dev artifact. Caveat: the script must live **inside the project dir** (ESM resolves `playwright` from local `node_modules`; a scratchpad copy throws `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`).
26

# Cross-Session Learnings — JasonVaughanComPortfolio

Non-obvious behaviors, validated patterns, and failure modes worth remembering. Skip the obvious; capture only what would change how a future session approaches a similar task.

## 2026-06-22 — Adding a ccusage-backed token source (Codex): three non-obvious traps

Wiring OpenAI Codex into the stats collector surfaced three things that would have bitten a future "add another agent's tokens" task. See project-assets #28/#29/#30.

- **ccusage's multi-agent support is per-package, not per-subcommand on the installed binary.** The README lists ~15 agents (Codex, OpenClaw, Copilot CLI…), but the globally-installed `ccusage` (20.0.14) only has the Claude Code source — `ccusage codex` errors with "Command not found". Codex support lives in `ccusage@latest` (or the deprecated `@ccusage/codex`). **Don't trust the README's capability list against the installed version — probe the actual binary, and standardize on `npx -y ccusage@latest <agent>`.**

- **`auth_mode` is the double-count discriminator for any agent that can auth two ways.** Codex via ChatGPT login (`auth_mode=chatgpt`) is subscription-billed and *invisible* to the OpenAI admin-API usage report → safely additive. API-key auth *is* in that report → adding it would double-count. The fix that turns this from a fragile comment into a contract: gate collection on `auth_mode` per machine (only count when `chatgpt`), so a future auth switch can't silently double-count. Generalizes to any new token source — check its billing path before stacking it on an existing `api` source.

- **A "deployed copy with a manual sync step" *will* drift — verify the scheduled artifact is the one you edited.** The launchd agent ran a hand-copied `~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh` snapshot, not the repo script; editing `local-agent/refresh.sh` in the repo did nothing on schedule (the copy had frozen out the Codex source for 2 days). The durable fix is a **self-deploying wrapper** (`run-agent.sh`: `git pull` → `install` script to a stable path → `exec` the copy). It also dodges the mid-run-mutation hazard of pointing launchd directly at a repo file that the script itself `git pull`s. Before assuming a local fix is live, confirm what the scheduler actually executes.

## 2026-06-24 — Collapsible page + deep-links: five traps shipped across PRs #83/#84/#85

Converting the whole page to collapsible dropdowns (+ per-card/section share links) surfaced things that would re-bite any "collapse sections" or "deep-link to a card" task.

- **Collapsing by *unmounting* silently breaks deep-links into nested content.** First pass unmounted closed sections (clean for animation), but then `#monad-1` (a card inside Research) had no DOM element to scroll to. Fix: keep collapsed content **mounted**, hide via CSS (`grid-template-rows: 0fr->1fr` + `inert`), and have the hash handler walk *up* from the target to its enclosing `[data-collapsible]`, open that, then scroll+flash. Always-mounted is also just the original page behavior -> no perf regression. Don't unmount anything a deep-link might target.
- **Wrapping a component that owns its own `id` in a wrapper that also sets that `id` = duplicate DOM ids (12 sections).** Critic caught it. Fix: carry the section id in a `data-collapsible="<id>"` *attribute* (the registry/handler reads it), and only render an actual DOM `id` on the wrapper when the inner content has none (`provideId`/`bodyInWrap`). Edge case that broke the first fix: Pipeline renders **no** inner id, so omitting the wrapper id left `#pipeline` with no target — needed an explicit `provideId`. Rule: exactly one element per id; decide where the id lives based on whether the child already provides it.
- **A passing "pulse class is applied" test masked an invisible flash.** The amber highlight is a `box-shadow` ring — visible on a bordered card, but on a large transparent `<section>` the ring sits at the page edges and reads as nothing. `pulsed=true` in Playwright was true yet the user saw no flash. For section-level links, flash a *defined* element (the dropdown **header panel**), not the section. Lesson: assert the *visible effect*, not just that the class/attr was applied.
- **`navigator.clipboard` is blocked on insecure origins** (plain-HTTP LAN/tailnet, e.g. the `100.x:3300` dev server) — `writeText` rejects and the copy button silently no-ops. Needs a secure-context guard + legacy `execCommand("copy")` textarea fallback. Recurring trap for anything tested over the tailnet rather than `localhost`/HTTPS.
- **Two git/dev-serving gotchas:** (1) Branching a follow-up fix off a branch that then got **squash-merged** makes the next PR `DIRTY` (it re-applies the already-merged changes) — fix with `git rebase --onto origin/main <old-tip> <branch>` to replay only the new commits. (2) `vite dev` serves the SPA fallback for `/share/<id>/` (trailing slash) but the real OG stub only at `/share/<id>/index.html`; the trailing-slash form looks broken locally while GitHub Pages serves it correctly — don't diagnose the share-stub redirect from a `vite dev` trailing-slash URL.

**Validated tooling:** for DOM/scroll/anchor/flash behavior that vitest (node, no jsdom) can't cover, a throwaway **Playwright** script driving the real dev server is the right verification — it caught the duplicate id, the scroll near-miss, and the `/share/` dev artifact. Caveat: the script must live **inside the project dir** (ESM resolves `playwright` from local `node_modules`; a scratchpad copy throws `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`).

## 2026-06-25 — Stats-delta badges + a stale-base review catch (PRs #86/#87/#88, project-assets #31)

Adding "/ 7d" delta badges to the last two bare BuilderStats tiles (AI Tokens, Projects Shipped) surfaced four things worth keeping.

- **A branch cut *before* a recent squash-merge shows phantom "new" changes in a three-dot diff — and the PR reviewer is what caught it.** `fix/card-share-links` predated #85's merge, so `git diff origin/main...HEAD` (three-dot, the GitHub PR view) listed the already-merged copy-link changes as if new; my PR description then advertised a feature that was already live. The **two-dot** `git diff origin/main HEAD` showed those files byte-identical to main. Fix: `git rebase origin/main` — git drops the duplicate commit by patch-id ("skipped previously applied commit"). Lesson: when a branch is behind a merge, trust two-dot over three-dot, and **don't skip the independent reviewer on "trivial" PRs** — this was invisible to me and obvious to a fresh-context agent. (Related to the 2026-06-24 DIRTY-PR note, but that one shows up as a merge status; this one hides inside a clean-looking diff.)
- **Make the consumer forward-compatible so paired cross-repo PRs merge in any order.** The badges needed a collector change (project-assets emits `aggregateDeltas.tokens`/`.projects`) *and* a portfolio change (read them). Portfolio already used `delta: d ? d.x : null`, so absent fields → undefined → badge hidden. That graceful-degrade let both PRs merge independently with zero coordination — no "merge collector first" dance. Default to this whenever a portfolio feature depends on a not-yet-shipped manifest field.
- **A new *delta calculation* over an already-archived field activates on the next run — only a brand-new field needs the 7-day-baseline wait.** I (and the user) assumed the tokens delta would need 7 days to "warm up." Wrong: `aggregateTokens.total` has been in the manifest for months, so the 7-days-ago baseline already existed in git history → delta computed immediately. The null-until-baseline guard (copied from `authored`) only matters for fields with no prior history. Don't promise a warm-up wait without checking whether the underlying value is already archived.
- **`.prawduct/.test-evidence.json` has no CLI writer — refresh it by hand when the PR reviewer flags it stale.** The reviewer checks the evidence file's `git_sha`/`branch` against HEAD; `product-hook` has `test-status`/`validate-evidence` but no *write* subcommand (a hook normally writes it). When it's stale (e.g. from a sibling branch), write the JSON matching the existing schema for the current sha, then `python3 tools/product-hook validate-evidence` to confirm `valid` + `current`.

# Cross-Session Learnings — JasonVaughanComPortfolio

Non-obvious behaviors, validated patterns, and failure modes worth remembering. Skip the obvious; capture only what would change how a future session approaches a similar task.

## 2026-06-22 — Adding a ccusage-backed token source (Codex): three non-obvious traps

Wiring OpenAI Codex into the stats collector surfaced three things that would have bitten a future "add another agent's tokens" task. See project-assets #28/#29/#30.

- **ccusage's multi-agent support is per-package, not per-subcommand on the installed binary.** The README lists ~15 agents (Codex, OpenClaw, Copilot CLI…), but the globally-installed `ccusage` (20.0.14) only has the Claude Code source — `ccusage codex` errors with "Command not found". Codex support lives in `ccusage@latest` (or the deprecated `@ccusage/codex`). **Don't trust the README's capability list against the installed version — probe the actual binary, and standardize on `npx -y ccusage@latest <agent>`.**

- **`auth_mode` is the double-count discriminator for any agent that can auth two ways.** Codex via ChatGPT login (`auth_mode=chatgpt`) is subscription-billed and *invisible* to the OpenAI admin-API usage report → safely additive. API-key auth *is* in that report → adding it would double-count. The fix that turns this from a fragile comment into a contract: gate collection on `auth_mode` per machine (only count when `chatgpt`), so a future auth switch can't silently double-count. Generalizes to any new token source — check its billing path before stacking it on an existing `api` source.

- **A "deployed copy with a manual sync step" *will* drift — verify the scheduled artifact is the one you edited.** The launchd agent ran a hand-copied `~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh` snapshot, not the repo script; editing `local-agent/refresh.sh` in the repo did nothing on schedule (the copy had frozen out the Codex source for 2 days). The durable fix is a **self-deploying wrapper** (`run-agent.sh`: `git pull` → `install` script to a stable path → `exec` the copy). It also dodges the mid-run-mutation hazard of pointing launchd directly at a repo file that the script itself `git pull`s. Before assuming a local fix is live, confirm what the scheduler actually executes.

## 2026-06-24 — Collapsible page + deep-links: five traps shipped across PRs #83/#84/#85

Converting the whole page to collapsible dropdowns (+ per-card/section share links) surfaced things that would re-bite any "collapse sections" or "deep-link to a card" task.

- **Collapsing by *unmounting* silently breaks deep-links into nested content.** First pass unmounted closed sections (clean for animation), but then `#monad-1` (a card inside Research) had no DOM element to scroll to. Fix: keep collapsed content **mounted**, hide via CSS (`grid-template-rows: 0fr->1fr` + `inert`), and have the hash handler walk *up* from the target to its enclosing `[data-collapsible]`, open that, then scroll+flash. Always-mounted is also just the original page behavior -> no perf regression. Don't unmount anything a deep-link might target.
- **Wrapping a component that owns its own `id` in a wrapper that also sets that `id` = duplicate DOM ids (12 sections).** Critic caught it. Fix: carry the section id in a `data-collapsible="<id>"` *attribute* (the registry/handler reads it), and only render an actual DOM `id` on the wrapper when the inner content has none (`provideId`/`bodyInWrap`). Edge case that broke the first fix: Pipeline renders **no** inner id, so omitting the wrapper id left `#pipeline` with no target — needed an explicit `provideId`. Rule: exactly one element per id; decide where the id lives based on whether the child already provides it.
- **A passing "pulse class is applied" test masked an invisible flash.** The amber highlight is a `box-shadow` ring — visible on a bordered card, but on a large transparent `<section>` the ring sits at the page edges and reads as nothing. `pulsed=true` in Playwright was true yet the user saw no flash. For section-level links, flash a *defined* element (the dropdown **header panel**), not the section. Lesson: assert the *visible effect*, not just that the class/attr was applied.
- **`navigator.clipboard` is blocked on insecure origins** (plain-HTTP LAN/tailnet, e.g. the `100.x:3300` dev server) — `writeText` rejects and the copy button silently no-ops. Needs a secure-context guard + legacy `execCommand("copy")` textarea fallback. Recurring trap for anything tested over the tailnet rather than `localhost`/HTTPS.
- **Two git/dev-serving gotchas:** (1) Branching a follow-up fix off a branch that then got **squash-merged** makes the next PR `DIRTY` (it re-applies the already-merged changes) — fix with `git rebase --onto origin/main <old-tip> <branch>` to replay only the new commits. (2) `vite dev` serves the SPA fallback for `/share/<id>/` (trailing slash) but the real OG stub only at `/share/<id>/index.html`; the trailing-slash form looks broken locally while GitHub Pages serves it correctly — don't diagnose the share-stub redirect from a `vite dev` trailing-slash URL.

Caveat: the script must live **inside the project dir** (ESM resolves `playwright` from local `node_modules`; a scratchpad copy throws `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`).

content.text:
## Result
Captured a 2026-06-25 entry (PRs #86/#87/#88, project-assets#31) with four learnings: the stale-base **three-dot-diff phantom-duplicate trap** caught by the independent reviewer (use two-dot diff + rebase), the **forward-compatible consumer pattern** that lets paired cross-repo PRs merge in any order, the **delta-activates-immediately-on-archived-fields** distinction (no 7-day warm-up needed), and the **manual `.test-evidence.json` refresh** workaround (no CLI writer exists).

message.content:
You are at the end of a development session. Update `.tangleclaw/memories/MEMORY.md` to record this session AND emit a structured summary the wrap pipeline parses for the wrap commit.

File-edit steps:
1. Read `.tangleclaw/memories/MEMORY.md` for existing structure (Boot pointer / Next Session / Last Session blocks).
2. Read `.tangleclaw/memories/wrap-log.md` if it exists — older session blocks get demoted there to keep MEMORY.md scannable.
3. Inspect session scope: `git log --oneline HEAD~10..HEAD` (or `<lastWrapSha>..HEAD` if `.tangleclaw/project.json` has a non-null `lastWrapSha`), `git status --short`.
4. Update MEMORY.md:
   - Demote the existing `Last Session` block (if any) into `wrap-log.md` (prepend to the file's most-recent-first section; create the file if absent).
   - Write a new `Last Session` block in MEMORY.md describing THIS session: what shipped, what was learned, what's next. Match the existing dated-heading convention.
   - Update the boot pointer's open-queue priorities if they shifted this session.
5. Save both files. The commit step picks them up via `git add -A`.

CRITICAL — structured summary FILE for pipeline parsing:

After saving MEMORY.md and wrap-log.md, WRITE A FILE at `.tangleclaw/.wrap-summary.md` containing EXACTLY these three `## Heading` blocks and nothing else. The wrap pipeline reads and then deletes this file — do NOT rely on chat output. Headings are matched case-insensitively against the literals `summary`, `nextSteps`, `learnings` — use these exact spellings (no spaces, no extra words):

## Summary
<2-3 sentence prose summary of this session, written as you'd write a commit subject — declarative, concise, present-tense>

## NextSteps
- <highest-priority next item>
- <second item>
- <additional items as needed>

## Learnings
- <non-obvious takeaway>
- <another takeaway>
- (or write `none` on a single line if routine)

Why a FILE and not chat: the wrap captures your tmux pane, but the Claude Code TUI renders `## ` headings without the literal `##` characters, so headings emitted to chat cannot be parsed and BLOCK the wrap (#287). Writing the block to `.tangleclaw/.wrap-summary.md` preserves the raw markdown. `lib/wrap-steps/ai-content.js` reads + parses that file against the step's `captureFields`; missing or empty blocks BLOCK the wrap with `Required captureField "<name>" missing or empty in AI response`. The `## Summary` content also flows into `_completeV2Wrap`'s summary deriver in `lib/sessions.js` and becomes the wrap commit subject line.

1	# Session Memory
2	
3	This file persists context across AI sessions. Update it with key decisions, progress, and open questions.
4	
5	## Last Session (2026-06-24 — Skills + Certifications sections + page-wide collapsible dropdowns)
6	
7	The long-deferred **portfolio interview** finally happened, then shipped end-to-end. All live on jasonvaughan.com via **portfolio PRs #83/#84/#85** (Critic-reviewed, Playwright-verified, all deploys green). See auto-memory `skills-certs-collapsible-shipped`.
8	
9	**Shipped:**
10	1. **Skills section** (`src/components/Skills.jsx` + `src/data/skills.js`, 6 tests) — creative/live-show/broadcast/IT pro tools GitHub can't surface (the differentiator). Domains: Media Servers (Millumin/Watchout/Pixera/Mitti/Q-Lab/Disguise), Screen Switchers (Barco E2/E3, Analog Way Ascender/Aquilon, Spyder X20), Broadcast/Signal Flow/**IT** (SMPTE-2110, Dante, fiber, IP networking, switching/routing, Cisco), Editing (Premiere/AE), Design (PS/AI), Audio (Pro Tools, live mixing). Optional `level` pill — **left blank by user choice**.
11	2. **Certifications section** (`src/components/Certifications.jsx` + `src/data/certifications.js`, 5 tests) — curated/current: 7-yr IATSE Local 16 stagecraft apprenticeship, Barco Video Eng Level A, OSHA 30, Google PM (Coursera, **2026** = currency signal), IATSE Training Trust Fund instructor. **Strategy decided: play the no-degree angle IN, never OUT** — never reference the degree's absence (it makes it the yardstick); NO hard anchor year (age-math risk); IATSE framed as *training*, never union advocacy.
12	3. **Collapsible page** (#83) — everything below Builder Stats is a dropdown (emoji icon + one-line descriptor + CSS grid-rows expand). `Collapsible.jsx` + `sectionRegistry.js`. Deep-links open the enclosing dropdown + flash the target (card flashes the card; **section links flash the header panel**, #84 — the box-shadow ring is invisible on a big transparent section).
13	4. **Share/deep-links** — card links = OG-rich `/share/<id>/`; section headers = clean `/#section`. **#85: added copy-links to every card that lacked one** (Projects, Pipeline/Medusa, ClawHub, GPTs). ShareLink clipboard now works on insecure tailnet HTTP (execCommand fallback).
14	
15	**Key facts / decisions:**
16	- **Internal-fork pattern validated:** deploy is **main-only** (GitHub Pages), so a feature branch IS the private staging — nothing's public until merged. Dev preview = vite on **port 3300**, host 0.0.0.0, over the tailnet.
17	- **Don't unmount collapsible content** a deep-link might target — keep mounted, CSS-hide (`grid-template-rows`+`inert`); the handler walks up to `[data-collapsible]`. Section id rides in the `data-collapsible` attr to avoid duplicate DOM ids (Critic finding). See learnings.md.
18	- **Playwright-from-inside-the-project** is the verification tool for DOM/scroll/flash behavior (vitest is node/no-jsdom). Scripts must live in the project dir for ESM `node_modules` resolution.
19	
20	**Open / next session:** **#81** show/hide refinement (partly addressed by collapsibles), **#82** cert + per-card OG-preview logos (new card links use plain `/#`, no social image), the minor **duplicate-`<h2>` trim** on wrapped sections (section shows its own heading + the dropdown label), and the lingering **accessibility/contrast pass** (WCAG AA fail on tag pills) + the **stale Cursor 7B** keep/retire decision.
21	
22	## Last Session (2026-06-17/18 — TangleBrain launch + hero card + Medusa positioning)
23	
24	**What happened:** Continued portfolio work. All shipped & merged (portfolio PRs #68–#72). Tests green (project-assets 46, portfolio 20).
25	
26	**Shipped:**
27	1. **TangleBrain** (new public repo, v0.10.0 — local-first config-driven LLM router) added to the portfolio. First as a Projects-grid card (#68), then **promoted to a featured HERO card** (`FeaturedTangleBrain.jsx`, #69) placed directly below FeaturedTangleClaw to group the "Tangle" siblings. Hero card uses live stats (`tanglebrain-stats.json`) + `useGitHubLatestRelease` hook + ShareLink (`/share/tanglebrain/`). Auto-rolled into BuilderStats totals (LoC now ~813k, authored ~1.33M).
28	2. **Logos** — project-assets is the **master asset store** (`<project>-logo.png` at repo root). Added `tanglebrain-logo.png` + refreshed `tangleclaw-logo.png` there (#25); portfolio keeps optimized 512px copies in `src/assets/projects/` (the masters are full-res 1254px). Hand-made logo art supplied by user.
29	3. **TangleBrain hero copy refactored** (#70) to match the upgraded README (problem-first; OAuth/local-first credentials — keys gated, never injected into a CLI; prompt-aware routing; "standalone or part of the Tangle family"). Drafted the README's "Problem/Solution" section and handed it to the TangleBrain repo session (cross-session boundary) — they shipped it.
30	4. **Medusa** — set GitHub repo description + 10 topics; portfolio card now cross-references TangleClaw PortHub (factual, README line 98) and reads as a **general coordination fabric** (parallel-sessions, fleet fan-out, cross-agent verification — each grounded in a real capability) (#71, #72). NOTE: Medusa↔UCI is provenance only (Medusa was spec'd from UCI originally) — NOT a runtime integration; do not claim one. Medusa's messy origin ("Bitch" project, near-abandonment) stays OFF the portfolio.
31	5. **Lines Refactored tooltip reframed** (#71) — anchored to Lines Authored (lifetime), not current LoC, so visitors don't misread refactored÷LoC (~66%) as "most code thrown away." Correct framing: refactored÷authored ≈ 40% lifetime churn = healthy iteration. (Not a red flag.)
32	
33	**Open / next-session:** none pending on the portfolio. Monad-1 publisher still stale (project-assets#15, owned by the Monad session). If a real Medusa↔UCI integration ships, add it to the Medusa card.
34	
35	## Prior Session (2026-06-13/14 — stats accuracy overhaul + Lines Authored + ClawHub downloads)
36	
37	**What happened:** Long multi-arc session, all shipped & merged. Both `jasonvaughan.com` and `project-assets` touched. Tests green in both (46 + 46).
38	
39	**Shipped:**
40	1. **Stats accuracy overhaul** — LOC now counts all source + HTML/CSS/Markdown (project-assets#11), dropped data/config exts after `.json` DB-dumps caused a false 11.8M reading (#12), removed TangleClaw's JS-only LOC override (now ~2x its LOC), portfolio tooltip parity (#59). LOC ~796k.

Notse Projects card switched from `#contact` to `/notse`. Internal `/`-prefixed links now stay in the same tab; anchors still smooth-scroll, external still opens new tab.

Customer-facing checkout reads "Notse License — $50.00/year".
64	3. **SSL cert fix on jasonvaughan.com** (no PR — Pages config) — Chrome was rejecting the site because the cert in the wild was GitHub's generic `*.github.io` (Let's Encrypt provisioning had silently failed for the custom domain). Fixed by clearing + re-adding the custom domain via the GitHub Pages API (`PUT /repos/.../pages` with `cname: null` then `cname: "jasonvaughan.com"`). Cert re-provisioned in seconds; SAN now covers `jasonvaughan.com` + `www.jasonvaughan.com`. `https_enforced: true`.
65	4. **Full automated licensing pipeline** (new `Jason-Vaughan/licensing-workers` private repo) — Cloudflare Worker bridges Stripe Payment Link checkouts → Keygen license creation → Resend email delivery. First Worker `notse-licensing` is live at `https://notse-licensing.jasonvaughan.workers.dev`. End-to-end smoke-tested: real Keygen license issued, key emailed to ntehosting@gmail.com. Resend sending domain `jasonvaughan.com` verified (SPF + DKIM + MX records in GoDaddy DNS — apex Outlook MX untouched). See `project_licensing_pipeline.md` auto-memory for full architecture + IDs.
66	5. **Tip Jar** (PR #22) — small amber pill above the H1 in the header (always-visible, low-pressure) + larger illustrated `TipJar.jsx` card between GPTs and ContactSection. Both link to one Stripe Payment Link with "customer chooses price" feature (`https://buy.stripe.com/7sY5kD6X8bUA7iNfEEaMU01`, product `prod_USrEuQMbhr1sFL`, `$5/$10/$25/$50/$100` preset suggestions, "Donate" call-to-action button). Inline SVG mason jar with bills + coins for the larger card.
67	6. **FR #23 filed** for crypto tipping option (Coinbase Commerce path). Not implemented — tracked for later.

69	**Workflow notes / patterns worth remembering:**
70	- The user has multiple Macs (Cursatory, elkaholic). Wrangler OAuth is per-machine and doesn't transfer. Workaround: use a Cloudflare API token (`Edit Cloudflare Workers` template) and export as `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` env var — works from any machine.
71	- The `workers.dev` subdomain registration is a one-time per-account step. If Cloudflare's UI sends you to a "Connect a domain" upsell page (paid plan picker), you're on the wrong page. Direct API path: `PUT /accounts/{id}/workers/subdomain` with `{"subdomain":"<name>"}`.
72	- DNS records in GoDaddy: enter just the subdomain part as `Name` (e.g., `send` not `send.jasonvaughan.com`). GoDaddy auto-appends the apex.
73	- Stripe Payment Link URLs are static — editing call-to-action ("Pay" → "Donate"), suggested amounts, etc. updates config on the same URL; URL never changes once created.
74	- Keygen distinguishes Account ID (UUID) from Stripe's account ID (`acct_...` prefix) — easy to confuse if you have both dashboards open.
75	- "DKIM key" ≠ "Resend API key" — DKIM is the public key in DNS, API key is for code → Resend authentication. Both come from Resend but are separate things.

Tile system extended with optional `description` field — when present, the tile renders with a dotted-underline cue + `cursor: help` and a hover popup explains the metric.
94	3. **`#15`** (fix) — first attempt at the 8-tile row hit `minmax(110px)` × 8 + gap = 964px > 856px container, wrapping. Bumped to `minmax(96px)` so 8×96 + 7×12 = 852 ≤ 856.
95	4. **`#16`** (fix) — user wanted tighter wrap point. Condensed: `minmax` 96→80, gap 12→10, value font 28→24, label font 11→10, letter-spacing 1→0.5. Wrap point dropped from ~960px viewport to ~810px viewport.

Each tooltip explains methodology (source, scope, non-obvious nuance), not just the label. The LOC tooltip notably clarifies "current snapshot vs lifetime-added" — addresses the recurring "why is the number lower than total commits added?" confusion.

User noticed the LOC tile shows ~350K which seemed lower than expected — explained: 477K (lifetime added) − 93K (deleted) = 384K net, then ~34K gap mostly from my ad-hoc count including CSS that per-repo LOC profiles exclude. The new LOC tooltip captures this snapshot-vs-lifetime distinction.

101	**Workflow notes:**
102	- Cron is 5x/day at `0 3,9,13,17,22 * * *` UTC. After merging the collector PR, manually triggered the workflow via `gh workflow run collect-stats --repo Jason-Vaughan/project-assets` so the new `aggregateRefactored` field landed within ~1 min instead of waiting for the next scheduled run. Pattern worth reusing when shipping a new manifest field.

Decoration tooltips don't earn their existence; methodology tooltips do. The dotted-underline + `cursor: help` cue signals "there's more to read here."

New regex `^(fix|bugfix|hotfix|fixed|fixes)([^a-zA-Z]|$)` is case-insensitive, subject-only (not body, which over-counts), zero false positives. **Production aggregateFixes: 142 → 284 (~11% of 2575 commits)** — healthy ratio for this body of work. 136	**More follow-ups (same session):**
137	- **PR #4 (project-assets):** stats workflow bumped from 1x daily to 5x/day, anchored to PT (`'0 3,9,13,17,22 * * *'` UTC). AI Tokens stat lags up to 24h since the local ccusage agent runs separately at 05:30 PT.

Plus new `TODO.md` at repo root.

156

Auto-discovers every Jason-Vaughan repo, computes LOC/tests/commits/contributors/languages, writes `_collect-meta.json` manifest. Daily cron 06:00 UTC + manual dispatch. All 8 source repos cleaned (workflows + PATs + committed stats.json removed).

161	2. **AI token aggregation** layered onto the same collector: pulls from Anthropic admin API (404s for personal accounts — falls back to local agent), OpenAI admin API (works), local agent JSON files for ccusage + Gemini telemetry, static `manual.<provider>` entries, and date-prorated formulas. Live total now ~9.3B tokens. New 5th headline stat in BuilderStats: "AI Tokens".

163	3. **Portfolio polish** — Cierre Sensei promoted to a 3rd hero card, Pipeline section added below Projects with two stages (Public Beta = Medusa, In Development = UCI), per-card live stats + auto language tags, status badges (Open Source / SaaS / Commercial / Archived), Notse marked closed-source, smooth-scroll for in-page anchor links.
164	
165	**Major deliverables (artifacts you can find):**
166	- `Jason-Vaughan/project-assets/.github/workflows/collect-stats.yml` (workflow), `projects.yml` (config), `scripts/collect-stats.mjs`, `scripts/lib/{git-stats,tokens}.mjs`, `scripts/counters/{tangleclaw,tilt}.mjs`
167	- `Jason-Vaughan/project-assets/local-agent/{refresh.sh,plist,gemini-telemetry-snippet.json,README.md}` — disaster-recovery copies of the local agent
168	- `~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh` (deployed) + `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.jasonvaughan.claude-stats.plist` (deployed) on Cursatory
169	- `~/.gemini/settings.json` updated with telemetry config
170	- Portfolio: new `Pipeline.jsx`, `FeaturedCierreSensei.jsx`, `utils/languageTags.js`; existing `BuilderStats`, `Projects`, `FeaturedProject`, `FeaturedTangleClaw` all extended

172	**Key decisions:**
173	- Self-discovery beats curated list: collector reads `/user/repos`, exclude list in `projects.yml` instead of include list
174	- Token aggregation is **additive** across source types per provider (api + agent + manual + prorated). Convention: never put the same usage in two source types
175	- Local agent on Cursatory pushes daily at 05:30 PT (before GitHub cron at 06:00 UTC); SSHs to habitat for habitat's ccusage data
176	- Cursor and TypingMind prepaid usage are static manual entries; ccusage and Gemini telemetry are auto-refreshed via local agent

All fixed in commits `71ddb96` (project-assets) and `928bafa` (portfolio).
186	
187	## Open items / next session
188	
189	- **Cursor entry is static** (7B from one-time CSV export). User said they don't use Cursor anymore so won't grow. Re-export and bump if usage resumes.
190	- **Stale `ci/stats-*` branches and `stash@{0}`** on TangleClaw / OnDeck-V9 / Notse — left intact per user request. Phase 5 cleanup deferred indefinitely.
191	- **OnDeck-V9 `ci/stats-workflow` -> main** — when user merges, remove the `branch: ci/stats-workflow` override line in `project-assets/projects.yml`.
192	- **rentalclaw-project** is currently auto-discovered (98 commits as of session end). User hasn't said whether to exclude it. If unrelated, add to `projects.yml#exclude`.
193	- **UCI repo** has 2 commits and the user is building it. Pipeline card has no link yet — once user wants public visibility, add `link: "https://github.com/Jason-Vaughan/UCI"` (verify exact slug) and `linkLabel: "View on GitHub"`.
194	- **Self-hosted LLM router (Kimi 2.5 / DeepSeek)** is on user's roadmap. Token tracking for it will use the `remoteStats` URL pattern when ready (~15 min wiring).
195	- **PAT rotation reminder**: `STATS_COLLECTOR_TOKEN` and `OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY` need manual rotation before they expire.
196	- **Color contrast**: critic flagged tag pill text (`#a1a1aa` on near-black) is below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for small text. Consistent across the site so leaving for a dedicated accessibility pass.
197	
198	## Lessons learned this session
199	
200	- **Critic review surfaces silent failures the builder misses** — the BLOCKER finding (gemini-usage.json never committed) was invisible from the builder's perspective because the file existed locally and the Anthropic flow worked. A run agent that tested both providers end-to-end would have caught it; a code review caught it instantly.
201	- **MultiSelect AskUserQuestion options must be independent items, not cumulative supersets** — already saved as a feedback memory.
202	- **Pasted secrets (PATs) are compromised the moment they hit the chat** — rotate immediately, even after they're added as repo secrets.
203	- **GitHub Pages deploy lag** is real — about 1-2 minutes from push to live. Cache-bust by appending `?$(date +%s)` when verifying changes via curl on raw.githubusercontent.com (and even that doesn't always work — GitHub caches by file hash).
204	- **macOS launchd at 05:30 PT vs GitHub cron at 06:00 UTC** means agent updates are at most ~24h behind GitHub stats, but they land within the same daily window. Acceptable for vanity stats.
205	
206	## Earlier sessions (preserved for context)
207	
208	### 2026-04-13 — Initial portfolio launch
209

TangleClaw hero card, Contact form (Web3Forms), ScrapeGoat + ClawBridge cards, TiLT stats corrected, Builder Stats bar, GitHub Pages deployment, custom domain (GoDaddy DNS → GitHub IPs, Let's Encrypt SSL). Stats system was per-repo `stats.yml` workflows in 8 repos with `PROJECT_ASSETS_TOKEN` for the 4 private ones. (All replaced this session.)
211	
212	### 2026-04-14 — Commits + "Building since" badge
213	
214	Added live commit count and month-of-first-commit badge to TiLT and TangleClaw hero cards. Refuctor stats handled gracefully via Promise.allSettled.
215	
216	### Email status
217	
218	Cancelled Google Workspace migration in April — addresses were "in use" but GW wouldn't say where. Signed up for Outlook via GoDaddy for 6-12 months. Goal: eventually resolve the conflict and migrate to GW.

# Session Memory

This file persists context across AI sessions. Update it with key decisions, progress, and open questions.

## Last Session (2026-06-24 — Skills + Certifications sections + page-wide collapsible dropdowns)

The long-deferred **portfolio interview** finally happened, then shipped end-to-end. All live on jasonvaughan.com via **portfolio PRs #83/#84/#85** (Critic-reviewed, Playwright-verified, all deploys green). See auto-memory `skills-certs-collapsible-shipped`.

**Shipped:**
1. **Skills section** (`src/components/Skills.jsx` + `src/data/skills.js`, 6 tests) — creative/live-show/broadcast/IT pro tools GitHub can't surface (the differentiator). Domains: Media Servers (Millumin/Watchout/Pixera/Mitti/Q-Lab/Disguise), Screen Switchers (Barco E2/E3, Analog Way Ascender/Aquilon, Spyder X20), Broadcast/Signal Flow/**IT** (SMPTE-2110, Dante, fiber, IP networking, switching/routing, Cisco), Editing (Premiere/AE), Design (PS/AI), Audio (Pro Tools, live mixing). Optional `level` pill — **left blank by user choice**.
2. **Certifications section** (`src/components/Certifications.jsx` + `src/data/certifications.js`, 5 tests) — curated/current: 7-yr IATSE Local 16 stagecraft apprenticeship, Barco Video Eng Level A, OSHA 30, Google PM (Coursera, **2026** = currency signal), IATSE Training Trust Fund instructor. **Strategy decided: play the no-degree angle IN, never OUT** — never reference the degree's absence (it makes it the yardstick); NO hard anchor year (age-math risk); IATSE framed as *training*, never union advocacy.
3. **Collapsible page** (#83) — everything below Builder Stats is a dropdown (emoji icon + one-line descriptor + CSS grid-rows expand). `Collapsible.jsx` + `sectionRegistry.js`. Deep-links open the enclosing dropdown + flash the target (card flashes the card; **section links flash the header panel**, #84 — the box-shadow ring is invisible on a big transparent section).
4. **Share/deep-links** — card links = OG-rich `/share/<id>/`; section headers = clean `/#section`. **#85: added copy-links to every card that lacked one** (Projects, Pipeline/Medusa, ClawHub, GPTs). ShareLink clipboard now works on insecure tailnet HTTP (execCommand fallback).

**Key facts / decisions:**
- **Internal-fork pattern validated:** deploy is **main-only** (GitHub Pages), so a feature branch IS the private staging — nothing's public until merged. Dev preview = vite on **port 3300**, host 0.0.0.0, over the tailnet.
- **Don't unmount collapsible content** a deep-link might target — keep mounted, CSS-hide (`grid-template-rows`+`inert`); the handler walks up to `[data-collapsible]`. Section id rides in the `data-collapsible` attr to avoid duplicate DOM ids (Critic finding). See learnings.md.
- **Playwright-from-inside-the-project** is the verification tool for DOM/scroll/flash behavior (vitest is node/no-jsdom). Scripts must live in the project dir for ESM `node_modules` resolution.

**Workflow notes / patterns worth remembering:**
- The user has multiple Macs (Cursatory, elkaholic). Wrangler OAuth is per-machine and doesn't transfer. Workaround: use a Cloudflare API token (`Edit Cloudflare Workers` template) and export as `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` env var — works from any machine.
- The `workers.dev` subdomain registration is a one-time per-account step. If Cloudflare's UI sends you to a "Connect a domain" upsell page (paid plan picker), you're on the wrong page. Direct API path: `PUT /accounts/{id}/workers/subdomain` with `{"subdomain":"<name>"}`.
- DNS records in GoDaddy: enter just the subdomain part as `Name` (e.g., `send` not `send.jasonvaughan.com`). GoDaddy auto-appends the apex.
- Stripe Payment Link URLs are static — editing call-to-action ("Pay" → "Donate"), suggested amounts, etc. updates config on the same URL; URL never changes once created.

- "DKIM key" ≠ "Resend API key" — DKIM is the public key in DNS, API key is for code → Resend authentication. Both come from Resend but are separate things.

## Lessons learned this session

- **Critic review surfaces silent failures the builder misses** — the BLOCKER finding (gemini-usage.json never committed) was invisible from the builder's perspective because the file existed locally and the Anthropic flow worked. A run agent that tested both providers end-to-end would have caught it; a code review caught it instantly.
- **MultiSelect AskUserQuestion options must be independent items, not cumulative supersets** — already saved as a feedback memory.
- **Pasted secrets (PATs) are compromised the moment they hit the chat** — rotate immediately, even after they're added as repo secrets.
- **GitHub Pages deploy lag** is real — about 1-2 minutes from push to live. Cache-bust by appending `?$(date +%s)` when verifying changes via curl on raw.githubusercontent.com (and even that doesn't always work — GitHub caches by file hash).

3	Older `Last Session` blocks demoted from `MEMORY.md` to keep it scannable. Most-recent first. The full canon for stats infrastructure lives in the auto-memory files (e.g. `project_stats_agent_tcc_fix`, `project_stats_system`).
4	
5	## Last Session (2026-06-22 — ccusage comparison → Codex token source + self-deploying stats agent)
6	
7	Started as a "compare ccusage vs what we do now" question; became a focused stats-pipeline session. All shipped & merged. **project-assets PRs #28/#29/#30** (issues #26/#27 closed); portfolio CHANGELOG + TODO direct to main. Token-counting work lives in project-assets; see auto-memory `project_stats_agent_tcc_fix` (updated this session).
8	
9	**Shipped:**
10	1. **OpenAI Codex now counted** (project-assets #28) — Codex CLI usage (~14.5M, previously **zero**) folded into the `openai` token line as an additive `agent` source via `ccusage codex` → new `codex-usage.json`. Codex here is **ChatGPT-auth** (`auth_mode=chatgpt`) → subscription-billed, invisible to the OpenAI admin API → **no double-count**. Enforced as a **contract**: `refresh.sh` only counts a machine's Codex when `auth_mode==chatgpt` (habitat has none → skips cleanly). Found that ccusage *already* fed our Anthropic number (it was source #2 all along).
11	2. **tokens.mjs hardened** (project-assets #29) — went **0 → 70 tests** (`tokens.test.mjs` new): injectable admin-API fetchers (`cfg.fetchers`), `sourceMix()` helper, `cfg.agentDir` test seam, `parse_total` stderr warnings on schema-change/zero-sum. Closed the api+agent double-count test blind spot the Critic flagged.
12	3. **AI Tokens daily lag fixed** (project-assets #30) — discovered the launchd agent ran a **stale hand-copied snapshot** (`~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh`, frozen 2 days, lacked Codex). Replaced with a **self-deploying wrapper `run-agent.sh`** (pull → `install` repo script + parser → `exec` stable copy). Bumped **1×→4×/day** (05:30/11:30/17:30/23:30 PT); the collector is actually **hourly**. Freshness ~24h → ~6h. Deployed + `launchctl`-reloaded + live smoke-tested on Cursatory.
13	
14	**Key facts:** Never hand-copy `refresh.sh` again (`run-agent.sh` self-deploys from the repo, fires 4×/day). Codex folds into the `openai` breakdown line; `ccusage codex` needs `ccusage@latest`. `auth_mode` is the double-count gate for any new two-way-auth token source. `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS` still empty by design (Monad double-count).
15	
16	## Last Session (2026-06-21/22 — stats-pipeline rescue + Antigravity + Notse downloads)
17	
18	Marathon session. Started as a "what's next" comparison, turned into a major infra + portfolio run. All shipped & merged. Portfolio PRs **#73–#79**; project-assets several direct-to-main commits + the daily collector re-run repeatedly.
19	
20	**Big wins:**
21	1. **Claude tokens were frozen 7 weeks** — the `~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh` launchd agent silently failed every run since 2026-04-28: macOS **TCC blocks launchd writing into `~/Documents`**. Fix: moved the project-assets clone to **`~/code/project-assets`** (compat symlink left at old path; `PROJECT_ASSETS` var repointed); made the Gemini telemetry parse **incremental** (byte-offset); reordered git (commit before `pull --rebase --autostash`). Headline **~9.3B → ~15.7B** (Claude 1.85B → ~8.2B). See auto-memory `project_stats_agent_tcc_fix`.
22	2. **Disk: ~88 GB freed.** Gemini `telemetry.log` was **72 GB** (re-logs its 39KB system prompt every event, no rotation) → trimmed to 10 GB + rolling cap (hysteresis 12/8 GB to avoid daily 10GB-rewrite thrash). Source of the gemini churn = a **forgotten Medusa A2A swarm** of long-lived `agy`/qwen agents (dormant since 6-12, now parked — Gemini OAuth dead anyway). Deleted **24 GB stale Apple Mail logs** + turned off `LogActivityToFile` (Mail shouldn't be on Cursatory). Freed space sits in Time Machine local snapshots (~24h to reclaim).
23	3. **Gemini CLI sunset → Antigravity.** Google killed Gemini CLI personal OAuth (migrate to Antigravity). Filed **TangleClaw#392**. Wired Antigravity (`agy`) token usage into the collector by reverse-engineering its SQLite `gen_metadata` protobuf → `meta.versions` + `meta.downloads`. New `fetchLatestReleaseInfo` + tests (project-assets 59/59).
24	4. **Collector hardened:** `js-yaml` ESM default-export break → named `import { load }`; **race-safe push** (rebase+retry — it shares main with the frequently-pushing agent).
25	5. **Volta double-count removed (#73).** Monad confirmed its `tokens.total` already counts all routed agent traffic (LiteLLM :4000 + Ollama tee :11435); **closed raw :11434 (their #61) → count airtight**. Emptied `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS`. Also fixed the original ~432k-tokens/$1 Monad-card mismatch. Principle saved: auto-memory `project_token_counting_principle` (count once at the router; Monad now, **TangleBrain when it meters**).
26	6. **Version sync (#74).** `/notse` hardcoded v0.5.12 while the card showed live v0.5.20. Now every surface reads `manifest.versions` (daily `fetchLatestRelease` → latest STABLE tag, configured in `projects.yml` `versions:`; independent of the stats exclude list so `notse-releases` works). `src/version-consistency.test.js` fails the build on any hardcoded `vX.Y.Z` in `public/**/*.html`.
27	7. **Notse downloads (#75–#79).** Collector captures release assets → `meta.downloads`. `/notse` hero has prominent **Download for Mac/Windows** buttons (centered, with a "two machines — download both" callout ABOVE them) + a **Quick start** section (sourced from notse-releases' GETTING-STARTED.md / release notes); project card has a **"Download both apps — the Prompter (Mac) and the Helper (Windows)"** labeled pill row. Both wired to `manifest.downloads.notse` → **always the latest build**, auto-updating on a new release.
28	
29	**Key facts:**
30	- project-assets clone is now at **`~/code/project-assets`** (NOT ~/Documents — TCC). The launchd stats agent self-heals daily 05:30 PT.
31	- Version chips + download links are **single-sourced from the collector manifest** (`versions` / `downloads`). To add a version/download for a new card: add `slug: Owner/Repo` to `projects.yml` `versions:`. NEVER hardcode versions (test enforces).
32	- `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS` is **empty by design** — routed agents double-count Monad. Don't re-add.
33

## Last Session (2026-06-22 — ccusage comparison → Codex token source + self-deploying stats agent)

Started as a "compare ccusage vs what we do now" question; became a focused stats-pipeline session. All shipped & merged. **project-assets PRs #28/#29/#30** (issues #26/#27 closed); portfolio CHANGELOG + TODO direct to main. Token-counting work lives in project-assets; see auto-memory `project_stats_agent_tcc_fix` (updated this session).

**Shipped:**
1. **OpenAI Codex now counted** (project-assets #28) — Codex CLI usage (~14.5M, previously **zero**) folded into the `openai` token line as an additive `agent` source via `ccusage codex` → new `codex-usage.json`. Enforced as a **contract**: `refresh.sh` only counts a machine's Codex when `auth_mode==chatgpt` (habitat has none → skips cleanly). Found that ccusage *already* fed our Anthropic number (it was source #2 all along).
2. **tokens.mjs hardened** (project-assets #29) — went **0 → 70 tests** (`tokens.test.mjs` new): injectable admin-API fetchers (`cfg.fetchers`), `sourceMix()` helper, `cfg.agentDir` test seam, `parse_total` stderr warnings on schema-change/zero-sum. Closed the api+agent double-count test blind spot the Critic flagged.
3. **AI Tokens daily lag fixed** (project-assets #30) — discovered the launchd agent ran a **stale hand-copied snapshot** (`~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh`, frozen 2 days, lacked Codex). Replaced with a **self-deploying wrapper `run-agent.sh`** (pull → `install` repo script + parser → `exec` stable copy). Bumped **1×→4×/day** (05:30/11:30/17:30/23:30 PT); the collector is actually **hourly**. Freshness ~24h → ~6h. Deployed + `launchctl`-reloaded + live smoke-tested on Cursatory.

**Key facts:** Never hand-copy `refresh.sh` again (`run-agent.sh` self-deploys from the repo, fires 4×/day). Codex folds into the `openai` breakdown line; `ccusage codex` needs `ccusage@latest`. `auth_mode` is the double-count gate for any new two-way-auth token source. `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS` still empty by design (Monad double-count).

## Last Session (2026-06-21/22 — stats-pipeline rescue + Antigravity + Notse downloads)

Marathon session. Started as a "what's next" comparison, turned into a major infra + portfolio run. All shipped & merged. Portfolio PRs **#73–#79**; project-assets several direct-to-main commits + the daily collector re-run repeatedly.

**Big wins:**
1. **Claude tokens were frozen 7 weeks** — the `~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh` launchd agent silently failed every run since 2026-04-28: macOS **TCC blocks launchd writing into `~/Documents`**. Fix: moved the project-assets clone to **`~/code/project-assets`** (compat symlink left at old path; `PROJECT_ASSETS` var repointed); made the Gemini telemetry parse **incremental** (byte-offset); reordered git (commit before `pull --rebase --autostash`). Headline **~9.3B → ~15.7B** (Claude 1.85B → ~8.2B). See auto-memory `project_stats_agent_tcc_fix`.
2. **Disk: ~88 GB freed.** Gemini `telemetry.log` was **72 GB** (re-logs its 39KB system prompt every event, no rotation) → trimmed to 10 GB + rolling cap (hysteresis 12/8 GB to avoid daily 10GB-rewrite thrash). Source of the gemini churn = a **forgotten Medusa A2A swarm** of long-lived `agy`/qwen agents (dormant since 6-12, now parked — Gemini OAuth dead anyway). Deleted **24 GB stale Apple Mail logs** + turned off `LogActivityToFile` (Mail shouldn't be on Cursatory). Freed space sits in Time Machine local snapshots (~24h to reclaim).
3. **Gemini CLI sunset → Antigravity.** Google killed Gemini CLI personal OAuth (migrate to Antigravity). Filed **TangleClaw#392**. Wired Antigravity (`agy`) token usage into the collector by reverse-engineering its SQLite `gen_metadata` protobuf → `meta.versions` + `meta.downloads`. New `fetchLatestReleaseInfo` + tests (project-assets 59/59).
4. **Collector hardened:** `js-yaml` ESM default-export break → named `import { load }`; **race-safe push** (rebase+retry — it shares main with the frequently-pushing agent).
5. **Volta double-count removed (#73).** Monad confirmed its `tokens.total` already counts all routed agent traffic (LiteLLM :4000 + Ollama tee :11435); **closed raw :11434 (their #61) → count airtight**. Emptied `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS`. Also fixed the original ~432k-tokens/$1 Monad-card mismatch. Principle saved: auto-memory `project_token_counting_principle` (count once at the router; Monad now, **TangleBrain when it meters**).
6. **Version sync (#74).** `/notse` hardcoded v0.5.12 while the card showed live v0.5.20. Now every surface reads `manifest.versions` (daily `fetchLatestRelease` → latest STABLE tag, configured in `projects.yml` `versions:`; independent of the stats exclude list so `notse-releases` works). `src/version-consistency.test.js` fails the build on any hardcoded `vX.Y.Z` in `public/**/*.html`.
7. **Notse downloads (#75–#79).** Collector captures release assets → `meta.downloads`. `/notse` hero has prominent **Download for Mac/Windows** buttons (centered, with a "two machines — download both" callout ABOVE them) + a **Quick start** section (sourced from notse-releases' GETTING-STARTED.md / release notes); project card has a **"Download both apps — the Prompter (Mac) and the Helper (Windows)"** labeled pill row. Both wired to `manifest.downloads.notse` → **always the latest build**, auto-updating on a new release.

**Key facts:**
- project-assets clone is now at **`~/code/project-assets`** (NOT ~/Documents — TCC). The launchd stats agent self-heals daily 05:30 PT.
- Version chips + download links are **single-sourced from the collector manifest** (`versions` / `downloads`). To add a version/download for a new card: add `slug: Owner/Repo` to `projects.yml` `versions:`. NEVER hardcode versions (test enforces).
- `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS` is **empty by design** — routed agents double-count Monad. Don't re-add.

content.text: Now I'll demote the 2026-06-24 Skills+Certs block into wrap-log.md (prepend, most-recent-first):

input.old_string: ## Last Session (2026-06-22 — ccusage comparison → Codex token source + self-deploying stats agent)
input.new_string:
## Last Session (2026-06-24 — Skills + Certifications sections + page-wide collapsible dropdowns)

The long-deferred **portfolio interview** finally happened, then shipped end-to-end. All live on jasonvaughan.com via **portfolio PRs #83/#84/#85** (Critic-reviewed, Playwright-verified, all deploys green). See auto-memory `skills-certs-collapsible-shipped`.

**Shipped:**
1. **Skills section** (`src/components/Skills.jsx` + `src/data/skills.js`, 6 tests) — creative/live-show/broadcast/IT pro tools GitHub can't surface (the differentiator). Domains: Media Servers (Millumin/Watchout/Pixera/Mitti/Q-Lab/Disguise), Screen Switchers (Barco E2/E3, Analog Way Ascender/Aquilon, Spyder X20), Broadcast/Signal Flow/**IT** (SMPTE-2110, Dante, fiber, IP networking, switching/routing, Cisco), Editing (Premiere/AE), Design (PS/AI), Audio (Pro Tools, live mixing). Optional `level` pill — **left blank by user choice**.
2. **Certifications section** (`src/components/Certifications.jsx` + `src/data/certifications.js`, 5 tests) — curated/current: 7-yr IATSE Local 16 stagecraft apprenticeship, Barco Video Eng Level A, OSHA 30, Google PM (Coursera, **2026** = currency signal), IATSE Training Trust Fund instructor. **Strategy decided: play the no-degree angle IN, never OUT** — never reference the degree's absence (it makes it the yardstick); NO hard anchor year (age-math risk); IATSE framed as *training*, never union advocacy.
3. **Collapsible page** (#83) — everything below Builder Stats is a dropdown (emoji icon + one-line descriptor + CSS grid-rows expand). `Collapsible.jsx` + `sectionRegistry.js`. Deep-links open the enclosing dropdown + flash the target (card flashes the card; **section links flash the header panel**, #84 — the box-shadow ring is invisible on a big transparent section).
4. **Share/deep-links** — card links = OG-rich `/share/<id>/`; section headers = clean `/#section`. **#85: added copy-links to every card that lacked one** (Projects, Pipeline/Medusa, ClawHub, GPTs). ShareLink clipboard now works on insecure tailnet HTTP (execCommand fallback).

**Key facts / decisions:**
- **Internal-fork pattern validated:** deploy is **main-only** (GitHub Pages), so a feature branch IS the private staging — nothing's public until merged. Dev preview = vite on **port 3300**, host 0.0.0.0, over the tailnet.
- **Don't unmount collapsible content** a deep-link might target — keep mounted, CSS-hide (`grid-template-rows`+`inert`); the handler walks up to `[data-collapsible]`. Section id rides in the `data-collapsible` attr to avoid duplicate DOM ids (Critic finding). See learnings.md.
- **Playwright-from-inside-the-project** is the verification tool for DOM/scroll/flash behavior (vitest is node/no-jsdom). Scripts must live in the project dir for ESM `node_modules` resolution.

## Last Session (2026-06-22 — ccusage comparison → Codex token source + self-deploying stats agent)

+2. **Certifications section** (`src/components/Certifications.jsx` + `src/data/certifications.js`, 5 tests) — curated/current: 7-yr IATSE Local 16 stagecraft apprenticeship, Barco Video Eng Level A, OSHA 30, Google PM (Coursera, **2026** = currency signal), IATSE Training Trust Fund instructor. **Strategy decided: play the no-degree angle IN, never OUT** — never reference the degree's absence (it makes it the yardstick); NO hard anchor year (age-math risk); IATSE framed as *training*, never union advocacy.

+3. **Collapsible page** (#83) — everything below Builder Stats is a dropdown (emoji icon + one-line descriptor + CSS grid-rows expand). `Collapsible.jsx` + `sectionRegistry.js`. Deep-links open the enclosing dropdown + flash the target (card flashes the card; **section links flash the header panel**, #84 — the box-shadow ring is invisible on a big transparent section).

+4. **Share/deep-links** — card links = OG-rich `/share/<id>/`; section headers = clean `/#section`. **#85: added copy-links to every card that lacked one** (Projects, Pipeline/Medusa, ClawHub, GPTs). ShareLink clipboard now works on insecure tailnet HTTP (execCommand fallback).

+- **Internal-fork pattern validated:** deploy is **main-only** (GitHub Pages), so a feature branch IS the private staging — nothing's public until merged. Dev preview = vite on **port 3300**, host 0.0.0.0, over the tailnet.

+- **Don't unmount collapsible content** a deep-link might target — keep mounted, CSS-hide (`grid-template-rows`+`inert`); the handler walks up to `[data-collapsible]`. Section id rides in the `data-collapsible` attr to avoid duplicate DOM ids (Critic finding). See learnings.md.
+- **Playwright-from-inside-the-project** is the verification tool for DOM/scroll/flash behavior (vitest is node/no-jsdom). Scripts must live in the project dir for ESM `node_modules` resolution.

Started as a "compare ccusage vs what we do now" question; became a focused stats-pipeline session. input.new_string:
## Last Session (2026-06-24/25 — ClawHub descriptor + 0.2.0 release cut + AI Tokens / Projects Shipped "/ 7d" delta badges)

Small-ask session that grew a cross-repo tail. All shipped & merged: **portfolio PRs #86/#87/#88** + **project-assets #31**. Tests green (portfolio 39). Delta-badge details + the cloud-only nuance now in auto-memory `project_stats_system` (extended); review/process learnings in `learnings.md`.

**Shipped:**
1. **ClawHub descriptor** (#86) — appended "— for the OpenClaw ecosystem" to the ClawHub dropdown's one-line description (`src/App.jsx`). The PR also **cut the 0.2.0 release** (CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]`→`[0.2.0]` + `package.json` 0.1.0→0.2.0) — that work rode in from the prior session's wrap commit.
2. **AI Tokens + Projects Shipped "/ 7d" delta badges** (#87 + collector **project-assets#31**) — the last two top-banner BuilderStats tiles without a week-over-week delta. Collector now emits `aggregateDeltas.tokens` (CLOUD only) + `aggregateDeltas.projects`; `BuilderStats` reads them. Also added a billions (`B`) branch to `formatDelta` (token delta is ~+6.2B → "+6.19B", not "+6191.9M"). Live: **AI Tokens +6.2B/7d**, Projects ±0.
3. **Projects Shipped always shows its badge** (#88) — per-tile `alwaysShowDelta` flag so it renders "±0 / 7d" in steady weeks (neutral gray, not red); every other tile keeps hiding zero deltas. Per user: "nobody ships products weekly," wants the field present-and-steady.

**Key facts / decisions:**
- **AI Tokens delta = CLOUD only.** Local inference (Monad-1 / fleet) is live-fetched in the browser, never archived in the manifest → no 7-day baseline → excluded from the delta. The *headline* number still = cloud+local. **This confused the user mid-session** — if asked, cloud IS counted (it's the part IN the delta); LOCAL is what's excluded from the delta. Documented in `project_stats_system`.
- **The tokens delta activated immediately, not after 7 days** — `aggregateTokens.total` was already archived for months, so the 7-days-ago baseline already existed. Only brand-new fields (like `authored` was) need a warm-up.
- **The PR reviewer caught a stale-base phantom-duplicate** on #86: branch was cut before #85 merged, so the three-dot `origin/main...HEAD` diff showed already-merged copy-link changes as "new." Two-dot diff revealed them byte-identical to main; `git rebase origin/main` dropped the dup by patch-id. Don't skip the independent reviewer on "trivial" PRs. See `learnings.md`.
- `.prawduct/.test-evidence.json` has **no CLI writer** — when the reviewer flags it stale (sibling-branch sha), hand-write the JSON for the current sha, then `product-hook validate-evidence`.

**Open / next session:** **Tag `v0.2.0`** — the release was cut in CHANGELOG but never tagged (`git tag -a v0.2.0` + `gh release create`). Current `[Unreleased]` (delta badges + descriptor) will roll into the next bump. Carried: **#82** cert/per-card OG-preview logos, **#81** collapsible show/hide refinement, the **duplicate-`<h2>` trim** on wrapped sections, **accessibility/contrast pass** (WCAG AA fail on tag pills), **stale Cursor 7B** keep/retire decision.

input.content:
## Summary
Ship the ClawHub "for the OpenClaw ecosystem" descriptor and cut the 0.2.0 release (#86), then add "/ 7d" delta badges to the AI Tokens and Projects Shipped BuilderStats tiles via a paired collector change (project-assets#31 + #87) and make Projects Shipped always render its badge (#88). The independent reviewer caught a stale-base phantom-duplicate on #86 that a rebase resolved; all four PRs merged with tests green.

## NextSteps
- Tag `v0.2.0` — the release was cut in CHANGELOG/package.json but never tagged (`git tag -a v0.2.0` + `gh release create --notes-from-tag`).
- #82 — add cert issuer logos + per-card OG-preview stubs (new card links use plain `/#`, no social image).
- #81 — collapsible-section show/hide refinement (partly addressed by the dropdown redesign).
- Trim the duplicate `<h2>` on wrapped sections (section renders its own heading plus the dropdown label).
- Accessibility/contrast pass (WCAG AA fail on tag-pill text) + the stale Cursor 7B keep/retire decision.

## Learnings
- A branch cut before a recent squash-merge shows phantom "new" changes in the three-dot `origin/main...HEAD` diff; verify with two-dot `origin/main HEAD` and `git rebase origin/main` to drop the dup by patch-id. The independent PR reviewer caught this — don't skip it on "trivial" PRs.
- Make the manifest consumer forward-compatible (`d ? d.x : null`) so paired cross-repo PRs (collector + portfolio) can merge in any order with no coordination.
- A new delta calculation over an already-archived field activates on the next run; only a brand-new field needs the 7-day-baseline warm-up. Don't promise a wait without checking whether the value is already in history.
- The AI Tokens delta is cloud-only (local inference is live-fetched, never archived) — a recurring point of confusion; the headline still shows cloud+local.
- `.prawduct/.test-evidence.json` has no CLI writer; when flagged stale, hand-write the JSON for the current sha then `product-hook validate-evidence`.
